before_code:
def mk2 (a b : ℕ+) : Fin 2 → ℕ+ := ![a, b]
def sigma1 (m : ℕ+) : ℕ := ...
description: VERY IMPORTANT: Avoid custom definitions where possible. Every def must be strongly justified and come with good API. Avoid small basic defs like sigma1 (use ArithmeticFunction.sigma) and mk2 (use ![a,b]). No custom notation when mathlib has one. Before creating ANY def, search mathlib exhaustively.

Each lemma/theorem must be separated by exactly one blank line. Fix ALL linter warnings: line length (100 char max), unused variables/hypotheses (prefix with _), unused simp args (remove from simp call). These are not optional.

before_code:
set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
private lemma foo : ... := by ...
after_code:
private lemma foo : ... := by
  -- break proof into helpers or pass typeclass args explicitly
description: maxHeartbeats options MUST be removed. Either break the proof into smaller helpers so each fits in default heartbeats, or if the issue is typeclass inference, pass arguments explicitly to avoid search. set_option maxHeartbeats is never acceptable.
Consecutive rw calls MUST be combined into a single rw [a, b, c]. Same applies to simp_rw. This is a basic golfing rule that /cleanup must always apply.

before_code:
private lemma foo
    (a : Fin n → ℕ)
    (ha : ∀ i, 0 < a i)
    (hdiv : DivChain n a) :
after_code: private lemma foo (a : Fin n → ℕ) (ha : ∀ i, 0 < a i) (hdiv : DivChain n a) :
description: Maximize use of the 100-char line width for declaration signatures. Do NOT put one parameter per line when multiple fit. Merge continuation lines starting with 4-space-indent '(' into the preceding line whenever combined length ≤ 99 chars.

Do not use verbose coercions like (↑g : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℚ).det when g.det or g.val.det suffices. Let Lean infer the coercion. Only add explicit type annotations when Lean actually fails to elaborate without them.

before_code:
lemma mk2_pos (a d : ℕ) (ha : 0 < a) (hd : 0 < d) : ∀ i, 0 < (![a, d]) i := by
  intro i; fin_cases i <;> simp [*]

-- 91 call sites: mk2_pos a d ha hd
after_code:
-- DELETE the wrapper. At call sites use:
(fun i => by fin_cases i <;> simp [*])
description: NEVER create named wrapper lemmas for trivial 1-line proofs like positivity of ![a,d] or DivChain of ![a,d]. Inline the proof at every call site. The cleanup/decompose skills must check EVERY definition and ask: does this need to exist? If the body is ≤3 lines of standard tactics (fin_cases, simp, omega), delete it and inline.

before_code: Matrix.diagonal (fun m => ((![1, p] : Fin 2 → ℕ) m : ℤ))
description: For 2×2 diagonal matrices, use mathlib's matrix literal notation !![a, 0; 0, d] instead of building Matrix.diagonal from a vector with pointwise coercion. The expression Matrix.diagonal (fun m => ((![a, d] : Fin 2 → ℕ) m : ℤ)) is unreadable — use !![a, 0; 0, d] directly.

before_code: def T_ad (a d : ℕ) (ha : 0 < a) (hd : 0 < d) (h : a ∣ d) : HeckeAlgebra 2
after_code: def T_ad (a d : ℕ) (h : a ∣ d) : HeckeAlgebra 2 := if 0 < a ∧ 0 < d then ... else 0
description: DEFINITIONS should NOT carry hypotheses like positivity or primality. Handle edge cases internally (return 0). Only require hypotheses in LEMMAS where they are actually needed for the proof.

after_code: T[a,d] for T_ad, T[n] for T[1,n], ⟨n⟩ₕ for diamond operator T[n,n]
description: Add scoped notation for common Hecke operators: T[a,d], T[n] = T[1,n], and diamond operator notation for T[n,n]. Makes the API readable and matches standard mathematical notation.

before_code:
noncomputable def diagMat (a : Fin n → ℕ) (ha : ∀ i, 0 < a i) : GL (Fin n) ℚ :=
  GeneralLinearGroup.mkOfDetNeZero (Matrix.diagonal (fun i => (a i : ℚ))) (by ...)

def diagDet (a : Fin n → ℕ) : ℕ := ∏ i, a i

def diagMul (a b : Fin n → ℕ) : Fin n → ℕ := fun i => a i * b i
after_code:
-- DELETE all three. Use mathlib directly:
-- GeneralLinearGroup.mkOfDetNeZero (Matrix.diagonal ...) at call sites
-- ∏ i, a i  directly
-- fun i => a i * b i  directly
description: EVERY definition must be scrutinized during cleanup, not just proofs. A def that composes 2-3 mathlib functions (like diagMat = mkOfDetNeZero ∘ Matrix.diagonal, or diagDet = Finset.prod, or diagMul = pointwise mul) is a FORBIDDEN wrapper. Delete it and use mathlib directly at call sites. The cleanup/decompose skills MUST check definitions, not just proofs. This was missed across multiple cleanup passes because agents only looked at proof structure.

before_code: diagMat n a ha -- custom wrapper around Matrix.diagonal + mkOfDetNeZero
after_code: Matrix.diagonal (fun i => (a i : ℚ)) -- use mathlib directly, wrap in GL only where needed
description: Use Matrix.diagonal directly instead of custom diagMat wrapper. Only wrap in GeneralLinearGroup.mkOfDetNeZero where a GL element is actually needed. Add API lemmas for Matrix.diagonal (det, integrality, etc.) rather than wrapping in a custom def. This is the next major refactoring target.

Plan for Shimura Theorem 3.20 (R_p ≅ Z[X₁,...,Xₙ]). Proof by induction on n using: (1) weight induction for surjectivity via Prop 3.17 (T_diag_scalar_mul) and Lemma 3.19 (forgetful map ψ: R_p^(n+1) → R_p^(n)), (2) algebraic independence via ψ and non-zero-divisor property of T(p,...,p). Key missing: ψ homomorphism, Ker(ψ) characterization, weight induction step. See Shimura pages 59-60.
